title: curcuma lutea
intro: Curcuma lutea, also known as Indian turmeric, is a perennial plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Curcuma lutea include reducing joint pain and inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a powder or oil, and can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and topical creams.
